*** 2 FATAL *** TWO MONTGOMERY COUNTY MEN WERE KILLED WHEN THEIR VEHICLE WAS SWEPT OFF THE ROAD NEAR RACKET CREEK NORTH OF ELK CITY LAKE, DURING A MULTI-HOUR PERIOD OF INTENSE RAINFALL. TIME IS ESTIMATED.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED VEHICLES ARE STRANDED.
MULTIPLE WATER RESCUES IN PROGRESS IN INDEPENDENCE AS WELL AS IN OTHER PARTS OF NORTHERN MONTGOMERY COUNTY.
WATER RESCUES CONTINUE IN THE CITY AS WELL AS ACROSS NORTHERN MONTGOMERY COUNTY. FLOODING ON SOUTH 10TH STREET IS 8 INCHES DEEP 1/4 MILE SOUTH OF INDEPENDENCE. SOUTH 10TH STREET IS CLOSED.
FLASH FLOODING CONTINUES IN INDEPENDENCE WITH 1-2 FEET OF WATER IN MANY AREAS. MAIN STREET AT 12TH TO 14TH IS NOW CLOSED AND PENNSYLVANIA 900 BLK IS CLOSED.
4 FEET OF WATER AT 13TH AND RAILROAD IN INDEPENDENCE. HOUSE RESCUES ARE UNDERWAY ON WEST BEECH STREET.
WATER RESCUES IN THE NW PART OF THE TOWN OF INDEPENDENCE ALONG WHISKEY CREEK. MULTIPLE REPORTS OF HOUSES FLOODING AND LAW ENFORCEMENT WAS GOING DOOR TO DOOR WARNING RESIDENTS.

FLASH FLOODING IN PROGRESS VERY CLOSE TO THE JUNCTION OF HIGHWAY 166 AND 59TH ROAD. FLASH FLOODING ALSO REPORTED ON HIGHWAY 160 1/4 MILE EAST OF ELK. BOTH LANES WERE FLOODED 6 INCHES DEEP OVER A 30-YARD STRETCH WHILE WEST BOUND LANE WAS FLOODED OVER A 100-YARD STRETCH.

WINDS FROM 50 TO 60 MPH WERE ALSO REPORTED.
THE FIRE DEPARTMENT INDICATED THAT THE EVENT OCCURRED 5 TO 10 MINUTES EARLIER, HENCE THE 603 PM CDT ESTIMATED TIME OF OCCURRENCE. PING PONG BALL-SIZED HAIL ALSO OCCURRED AT THE TIME.
THE FIRE DEPARTMENT INDICATED THAT THE EVENT OCCURRED 5 TO 10 MINUTES EARLIER, HENCE THE 603 PM CDT ESTIMATED TIME OF OCCURRENCE. WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 60 MPH ALSO OCCURRED AT THE TIME.

SEVERAL ROADS ARE CLOSED IN MOSTLY RURAL AREAS AROUND KINGMAN COUNTY. THERE ARE CONCERNS BY THE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER THAT RUSHING WATER MAY HAVE ERODED SOME OF THEM AND BARRICADES ARE BEING PUT UP.
